RT @TheAliceSmith: @elonmusk @deafneanderthal Well, it worked with Charlie Kirk, didn’t it?

I've increasingly noticed Claude going out of its way to pass judgment on things without being asked.

For example - I'll sometimes ask it to review a tweet draft and suggest sharper framing.

And I get a long rant about why I shouldn't tweet the take because I might be wrong 🙃

Most recent example:

Claude did NOT want me to tweet something positive about Midjourney Medical.

It initially told me this was because the "domain experts" (radiologists) had concerns, and so it wanted to protect me...

but later admitted it was just a value judgment 🫠 https://t.co/BIioh6VSKr

Why going direct is about telling a story much bigger than your company's:

@pmarca: "The story of you and your startup is not inherently an interesting story, but there is almost certainly an interesting story that involves your startup, and this is sort of the cheat code of https://t.co/LBGs93fGOW
